creator: Alexander Terp
description:
The header comment in rts/rl/errors.go instructs: "When adding here, also touch core/error_docs/<code>.md and the reference at docs-web/docs/reference/errors.md." But docs-web/docs/reference/errors.md does not exist - recent error-code additions (e.g. RAD40013, RAD40014) only added the embedded core/error_docs/<code>.md and skipped the reference.

Decide and do one of:
  1. Create docs-web/docs/reference/errors.md as the user-facing error reference - ideally generated/aggregated from the embedded core/error_docs/*.md files (single source of truth) rather than hand-maintained, and wire it into the docs-web site nav. A generator would mirror how funcs docs work.
  2. If a separate web reference isn't wanted, update the errors.go header comment to stop pointing at a nonexistent file so it stops misleading contributors.

Context: surfaced while implementing RAD40014 (unknown-map-key diagnostic). I added core/error_docs/40014.md but intentionally skipped the nonexistent reference, matching the 40013 precedent.
title: Resolve docs-web errors.md reference (missing file)
